task: Find healthy savory vegan snack recipes which can be cooked within 5 minutes and contain a high level of protein.
graderReasoning: The agent completed the information-finding task on Healthline and returned a supported list of healthy savory vegan snack recipes/options that can be prepared in about 5 minutes and are relatively high in protein. Evidence in the trajectory shows the agent visited the relevant Healthline page '24 Healthy Vegan Snack Ideas' and extracted specific qualifying items such as Edamame with Sea Salt (close to 17 g protein per cup; microwave prep), White Bean Dip, and Popcorn with Nutritional Yeast. It also used Healthline's '30 High Protein Snacks That Are Healthy and Portable' page to support additional quick vegan high-protein options like lentil salad, pumpkin seeds, and peanut butter celery sticks. The final answer clearly filtered for savory, vegan, quick-prep items and cited Healthline-backed protein amounts and prep methods. Although some items are more 'assembled' than 'cooked,' this is a reasonable interpretation of the user's request for quick snack recipes, and the core task of finding suitable Healthline options was accomplished.
final_result_response:
I re-read the two Healthline pages and pulled the savory vegan options that can be made in about 5 minutes, using microwave, no-cook assembly, or already cooked/canned ingredients.

| Best fit | Protein level from Healthline | Why it works | 5-minute prep |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **Edamame with sea salt or soy sauce** | **Close to 17 g per cup** | Healthline calls edamame an “excellent source of high-quality plant protein” and says it can be microwaved, then seasoned with sea salt or soy sauce. <citation refs="fetch_call_oxkHBru3D72qv1jXBCmirlY5|fc_056a320500c5976f016a050001e660819998b7af5769955b15#1">“One cup (155 grams) provides close to 17 grams of protein… prepare edamame… by thawing them in your microwave. Sprinkle… with sea salt or soy sauce.”</citation> | Microwave frozen edamame 3-5 min, drain, season. |
| **Lentil salad cup** | **18 g per cup cooked lentils** | Savory, vegan, very high protein if you use canned or pre-cooked lentils. <citation refs="fetch_call_IMBAy2i59xRP0OKeXUZLyHf8|fc_056a320500c5976f016a05002f96908199a99753fd30c79e3e#1">Healthline says lentil salad is a plant-based protein snack and “1 cup of cooked lentils provides 18 g of protein.”</citation> | Rinse canned lentils, mix with chopped cucumber/tomato, olive oil, vinegar/lemon, herbs, salt, pepper. |
| **White bean dip with raw vegetables** | **About 5 g per 1/4 cup white beans** | Savory, vegan, no-cook if using canned cannellini beans. <citation refs="fetch_call_oxkHBru3D72qv1jXBCmirlY5|fc_056a320500c5976f016a050001e660819998b7af5769955b15#1">Healthline says white bean dip is made by blending white/cannellini beans with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and herbs, and white beans pack “approximately five grams of protein… in just 1/4 cup.”</citation> | Blend canned beans, lemon, garlic, olive oil, herbs. Eat with celery, cucumber, carrots, radishes. |
| **Peanut butter celery sticks** | **7 g per 2 tbsp peanut butter** | Savory-leaning if you skip raisins. Healthline lists peanut butter celery sticks as an easy high-protein snack. <citation refs="fetch_call_IMBAy2i59xRP0OKeXUZLyHf8|fc_056a320500c5976f016a05002f96908199a99753fd30c79e3e#1">Healthline: peanut butter provides “7 g… per 2-tbsp. (30-g) serving.”</citation> | Spread peanut butter on celery, add chili flakes or sesame seeds instead of raisins. |
| **“Cheesy” nutritional yeast popcorn** | **3 g per 2 tbsp nutritional yeast** | Savory, vegan, fast, but lower protein than edamame or lentils. Healthline includes it as a high-protein snack idea. <citation refs="fetch_call_IMBAy2i59xRP0OKeXUZLyHf8|fc_056a320500c5976f016a05002f96908199a99753fd30c79e3e#1">Healthline recommends “3 cups of popcorn with 2 tbsp. of nutritional yeast and a pinch of salt,” noting nutritional yeast provides 3 g protein per 2 tbsp.</citation> | Microwave/air-pop popcorn, toss with nutritional yeast, salt, garlic powder, smoked paprika. |
| **Spiced pumpkin seeds** | **8.5 g per ounce** | Savory, vegan, high-protein, best if using raw or pre-roasted seeds. <citation refs="fetch_call_IMBAy2i59xRP0OKeXUZLyHf8|fc_056a320500c5976f016a05002f96908199a99753fd30c79e3e#1">Healthline: “One ounce (28 g) of pumpkin seeds contains 8.5 g of protein.”</citation> | Toss pumpkin seeds with smoked paprika, garlic powder, cayenne, and a pinch of salt. |

**Best overall pick:** **microwave edamame with sea salt or soy sauce**. It’s the strongest match for savory, vegan, high-protein, and truly cookable in under 5 minutes.
action_history: Opening Healthline vegan snacks: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/vegan-snacks');
